contents.title: IELTS English Podcast - Speaking Topic: Technology

vocabulary.definition: The subjects that are taught in a school, college, etc., or the things that are studied in a particular subject.
examples: The school has a very broad curriculum.
The curriculum includes history, science, and mathematics.
vocabulary.context_sentence: this is a 40 topic curriculum.

vocabulary.definition: A time of great success, happiness, or prosperity.
examples: The company is enjoying a golden period of growth.
The 1920s were a golden period for jazz music.
vocabulary.context_sentence: But for me right now, this is a real golden period.

vocabulary.definition: A person who is enthusiastic about new technology.
examples: He is a true technophile, always eager to try the latest gadgets.
Technophiles often embrace technological advancements quickly.
vocabulary.context_sentence: The first group are called technophiles.

vocabulary.definition: A person who dislikes or fears new technology.
examples: My grandmother is a technophobe and struggles with computers.
Technophobes may avoid using new technologies due to fear or lack of understanding.
vocabulary.context_sentence: The second group are called technophobes.

vocabulary.definition: A small, useful machine or device.
examples: I have a lot of gadgets in my kitchen.
This new gadget can translate languages in real time.
vocabulary.context_sentence: What gadgets do you use on a daily basis?

vocabulary.definition: Without any delay; instantly.
examples: I knew immediately that something was wrong.
The doctor told me to call him immediately if the symptoms worsened.
vocabulary.context_sentence: The ones that come to my mind immediately are my phone, my laptop,

vocabulary.definition: To improve something or make it more modern.
examples: I decided to upgrade my computer.
The company is planning to upgrade its software.
vocabulary.context_sentence: but last year I decided to upgrade to kind of a top of the range.

vocabulary.definition: Using or based on intuition; readily learned or understood.
examples: The software has an intuitive interface.
The design is intuitive and easy to use.
vocabulary.context_sentence: You know, it's quite an intuitive design, so it's quite easy to learn how to use it,

vocabulary.definition: The process or art of making videos.
examples: He has a passion for videography.
She is studying videography at university.
vocabulary.context_sentence: because as I said, I do like videography, particularly, I want to take good videos of my daughter

vocabulary.definition: No longer produced or used; out of date.
examples: Many traditional farming methods have become obsolete.
The technology has quickly become obsolete.
vocabulary.context_sentence: A lot of other technology became obsolete.

vocabulary.definition: A new idea, method, or thing that completely changes the way that something is done.
examples: The invention of the printing press was a game changer.
This new technology is a real game changer for the industry.
vocabulary.context_sentence: because it was a real game changer.

vocabulary.definition: The state of depending on or trusting in something or someone.
examples: Our reliance on fossil fuels is unsustainable.
There is an increasing reliance on technology in education.
vocabulary.context_sentence: Another one that comes to my mind is our reliance on maps

vocabulary.definition: A thing that prevents someone from concentrating on something else.
examples: The noise was a distraction.
Social media can be a major distraction for students.
vocabulary.context_sentence: I find that phones are a distraction.

grammar.title: Present Perfect Tense
grammar.explanation: Used to describe an action that started in the past and continues to the present or has a result in the present.
examples: I have lived here for five years.
She has finished her homework.
grammar.context_sentence: I've always had kind of okay phones, but last year I decided to upgrade

grammar.title: Past Perfect Tense
grammar.explanation: Used to describe an action that was completed before another action in the past.
examples: I had finished the book before the movie came out.
She had already eaten when I arrived.
grammar.context_sentence: I've had several phones, and I've liked some of them, I've always used Samsung.

grammar.title: Use of 'Whether' to introduce alternatives
grammar.explanation: Used to introduce two or more possibilities.
examples: I don't know whether I should go or stay.
She's not sure whether to buy the red dress or the blue one.
grammar.context_sentence: Whether it's work, I'm using my laptop, my phone equipment for my podcast or sports.

grammar.title: Use of 'For example' to introduce examples
grammar.explanation: Used to introduce a specific instance to illustrate a point.
examples: For example, the price of gasoline has increased.
For example, many people enjoy playing sports.
grammar.context_sentence: For example, people rely on modern technology for writing

grammar.title: Use of 'because' to express reason
grammar.explanation: Used to introduce the reason for something.
examples: I am happy because I passed the exam.
She is tired because she worked all day.
grammar.context_sentence: I think the the biggest technological development that comes to my mind is the phone, because it was a real game changer.

grammar.title: Use of 'so' to express result
grammar.explanation: Used to introduce the result or consequence of something.
examples: It was raining, so I took an umbrella.
He was tired, so he went to bed early.
grammar.context_sentence: So I was excited to have this that I can carry around with me day to day.

grammar.title: Use of 'not only...but also'
grammar.explanation: Used to add information and emphasize a point.
grammar.structure: Not only + auxiliary verb + subject + ... but also ...
examples: She is not only intelligent but also kind.
He not only speaks English but also French.
grammar.context_sentence: And it didn't disappoint. I was really impressed.

contents.title: Nail Your Opinion Essay in 17 Minutes: IELTS Writing Task 2 (Agree or Disagree)

vocabulary.definition: difficult in an interesting or enjoyable way
examples: The exam was challenging.
It's a challenging task to learn a new language.
vocabulary.context_sentence: I've selected quite a challenging topic that was used in real exams

vocabulary.definition: used to ask or talk about how much something is true or how great something is
examples: To what extent do you agree with this statement?
To what extent is climate change caused by humans?
vocabulary.context_sentence: to what extent do you agree or disagree so this is an opinion essay

vocabulary.definition: to keep something at the same level or standard
examples: He maintained his composure despite the pressure.
The company is struggling to maintain its market share.
vocabulary.context_sentence: your ability to maintain a good point of view

vocabulary.definition: urgent; needing to be dealt with immediately
examples: The government is facing pressing economic problems.
There are pressing issues that need to be addressed.
vocabulary.context_sentence: that even though there are pressing problems on Earth this is still money well spent

vocabulary.definition: to change the use or purpose of something
examples: The government is diverting funds from education to defense.
The company diverted resources to a new project.
vocabulary.context_sentence: I firmly believe that diverting financial resources from space exploration would be a mistake

vocabulary.definition: an advantage or profit gained from something
examples: The benefits of exercise are well-known.
The company offers a range of benefits to its employees.
vocabulary.context_sentence: these projects have brought and will continue to bring great benefits to humanity

vocabulary.definition: to express the meaning of something using different words, especially to achieve greater clarity
examples: The student was asked to paraphrase the passage.
Paraphrasing can help you understand the text better.
vocabulary.context_sentence: In the first notice the paraphrasing spend too much money on Space research becomes the millions

vocabulary.definition: needing immediate attention
examples: The situation requires urgent action.
The doctor said the patient needed urgent care.
vocabulary.context_sentence: would be better invested in addressing urgent issues on this planet

vocabulary.definition: to make an action or process easier
examples: The new software facilitates communication.
The teacher facilitated a discussion among the students.
vocabulary.context_sentence: have not only facilitated scientific advances in areas such as weather monitoring and navigation

vocabulary.definition: very great or intense
examples: The experience had a profound effect on her.
The philosopher's ideas were profound.
vocabulary.context_sentence: we have witnessed profound changes in the way we connect with each other

vocabulary.definition: connected with each other
examples: The world is becoming increasingly interconnected.
The internet has interconnected people all over the world.
vocabulary.context_sentence: satellite technology has interconnected the world shaping the way we live today

vocabulary.definition: a useful result of an activity designed to produce something else
examples: Many spin-offs have resulted from space exploration.
The company developed a spin-off product from its main line.
vocabulary.context_sentence: these spinoffs cover a wide range from comfortable memory phone mattresses to Tiny cameras in Mobile

vocabulary.definition: to produce or provide something
examples: The research yielded some surprising results.
The investment is expected to yield a good return.
vocabulary.context_sentence: how Innovations originally intended for space exploration continue to yield practical benefits

vocabulary.definition: the action of spending funds
examples: Government expenditure on healthcare has increased.
The company's expenditure was higher than expected.
vocabulary.context_sentence: there is no doubt that the expenditure on Space research is enormous

grammar.title: To what extent questions
grammar.explanation: Understanding the structure and requirements of 'To what extent do you agree/disagree' essay questions.
grammar.structure: Question: To what extent do you agree or disagree?
examples: To what extent do you agree with the statement?
To what extent is this a problem?
grammar.context_sentence: to what extent do you agree or disagree so this is an opinion essay

grammar.title: Complex Sentences with 'Although'
grammar.explanation: Using 'although' to create complex sentences and show contrast.
examples: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.
Although she was tired, she kept working.
grammar.context_sentence: Although there are undoubtedly many problems on Earth that need tackling I firmly believe that diverting financial resources from space exploration would be a mistake

grammar.title: Present Perfect Tense
grammar.explanation: Using the present perfect tense to show the ongoing effects of past actions.
examples: I have lived here for five years.
She has finished her homework.
grammar.context_sentence: to show how developments which began some time ago continue to have effect on us today

grammar.title: Using Present Participles
grammar.explanation: Using present participles (-ing) to combine sentences and add detail.
examples: She walked to the store, buying some milk.
He studied hard, passing the exam.
grammar.context_sentence: because of satellites we have seen big changes in how we connect with each other comma using the internet

grammar.title: Not only... but also...
grammar.explanation: Using 'not only... but also...' to present two related ideas.
grammar.structure: Not only + auxiliary verb + subject + but also...
examples: Not only is he intelligent, but he is also kind.
She is not only a talented singer, but also a great dancer.
grammar.context_sentence: satellites orbiting the Earth have not only facilitated scientific advances but have also transformed our daily lives

grammar.title: Referencing
grammar.explanation: Using pronouns and demonstratives to refer back to previous ideas.
examples: This is a good example.
These are the results.
grammar.context_sentence: be careful to use referencing in your essay because examiners check if you do now

grammar.title: Linking words
grammar.explanation: Using linking words to connect ideas between sentences and paragraphs.
examples: However, the results were different.
Furthermore, the study showed...
grammar.context_sentence: Furthermore carton AG Technologies initially developed for space exploration have been successfully adapted and turned into every typ products we use on Earth
